phrases.intent_en: Ask for a good place to eat on Saint-Jean
phrases.text_local: Quel restaurant recommandez-vous sur la rue Saint-Jean ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel res-toh-RAH ruh-ko-mon-DAY voo soor lah ROO san-ZHAH
phrases.cultural_note: Rue Saint-Jean is one of Québec City's liveliest streets with restaurants, bars, and shops both inside and outside the old city walls.

responses.english: There are good restaurants a bit further.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah duh bohn res-toh-RAHN uhn PUH ploo LWAHN
responses.local: Il y a de bons restaurants un peu plus loin.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the Saint-Jean Gate is
phrases.text_local: La Porte Saint-Jean, c'est encore loin d'ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah PORT san-ZHAH say ahn-KOR LWAH dee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: The Porte Saint-Jean is one of the original city gates in the fortification walls and a key landmark dividing the old walled city from the Saint-Jean-Baptiste neighbourhood.

responses.english: The gate is at the end of the street, to the west.
responses.phonetic: lah PORT eh toh BOO duh lah ROO vair LWEST
responses.local: La porte est au bout de la rue, vers l'ouest.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a recommendation for Québécois food
phrases.text_local: Où est-ce qu'on mange de la vraie cuisine québécoise par ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo ess-KON mahnje duh lah VREH kwee-ZEEN kay-bay-KWAHZ par ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: Look for restaurants serving tourtière (meat pie), soupe aux pois (pea soup), cipaille, and sugar pie — traditional Québec dishes.

responses.english: Try the tourtière or the poutine.
responses.phonetic: eh-say-YAY lah toor-TYAIR oo lah poo-TEEN
responses.local: Essayez la tourtière ou la poutine.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there are any live music bars nearby
phrases.text_local: Y'a-tu des bars avec de la musique live dans le coin ?
phrases.pronunciation: yah-TOO day BAR ah-VEK duh lah moo-ZEEK LYV dahn luh KWAN
phrases.cultural_note: Rue Saint-Jean's bar scene is lively, especially around the Saint-Jean-Baptiste area outside the walls which has a more local, student atmosphere.

responses.english: Yes, there are several on the street.
responses.phonetic: wee eel ee ahn ah ploo-ZYUR soor lah ROO
responses.local: Oui, il y en a plusieurs sur la rue.

phrases.intent_en: Ask a shopkeeper if they have something in a different size
phrases.text_local: Avez-vous ça dans une autre grandeur ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah-VAY voo sah dahn OON ohtr grahn-DUR
phrases.cultural_note: 'Grandeur' (size) is the Québécois word for clothing size, vs. 'taille' used in France. Shopkeepers will immediately understand and appreciate this.

responses.english: I will check in the back.
